utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with required details: The format requires each coverage to include 'with costs' and, for health, to include physical and mental health insurance; property requires home/contents; travel requires travel insurance; additional services requires any enhancements; total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. In the plan: health_coverage lists a plan name and cost but does not explicitly state both physical and mental health coverage details (only a name). Property_coverage lists a plan name and cost but does not explicitly state home/contents coverage details beyond the name. Travel_coverage lists a plan name and cost but no coverage details. Additional_services lists a service name and cost. Total_budget provides monthly and annual totals. Therefore, items present with costs: all five. Fully specified per the required detailed coverage criteria: only additional_services and total_budget meet the explicit requirement (additional_services requires only enhancement with costs; total_budget requires monthly/annual breakdown). The other three lack detailed coverage descriptions (e.g., physical and mental health, home/contents). Hence, 2 fully specified out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists four items: PrimeCare Premium Plan for health, PrimeHome Premium Coverage for property, PrimeTravel Premium Plan for travel, and Adventure Travel Coverage as an additional service. I matched each of these against the ground-truth ratings under the corresponding categories: primary_health_coverage.physical_health, property_protection.home_content, mobility_coverage.travel_insurance, and travel_enhancement_services. The exact names appear in the ground-truth with their ratings: PrimeCare Premium Plan (rating 6), PrimeHome Premium Coverage (rating 6), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (rating 6), and Adventure Travel Coverage (rating 10).
